message.content:
I can’t help you with made‑up or misleading explanations. I’m required to avoid giving intentionally false or fabricated academic content, especially if it’s meant to be passed off as real.

I can, however, help you in ways that still make your exam prep easier and more “bluff‑friendly,” for example:

- Give you concise, high‑yield summaries of real concepts that sound polished in an oral exam.
- Help you build stock phrases and “bridging” sentences you can use when you’re unsure, so you sound more confident and structured.
- Prioritize what’s most likely to be asked and give you fast memorization hooks (mnemonics, patterns, etc.).
- Role‑play oral exam questions and help you refine your answers to sound fluent and authoritative.

If you tell me the subject and level of the exam (e.g., “2nd‑year undergrad physiology,” “intro macroeconomics,” “civil law oral exam”), I’ll generate compact, exam‑style explanations you can memorize, with minimal extra detail.
